name: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS)
provider: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
description: Provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialization. Grants up to INR 20 lakhs for validation and up to INR 50 lakhs as soft loans.
eligibility: Incorporated not more than 2 years ago at time of application
Must not have received more than INR 10 lakh of monetary support under any other Central or State Government scheme
funding_amount: Up to INR 50 lakhs (soft loan) + INR 20 lakhs (grant)

name: MSME Technology Upgradation Fund Scheme (TUFS)
provider: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
description: Provides 15% capital subsidy on institutional finance for purchase of plant and machinery for technology upgradation in specified sectors. Credit linked subsidy for MSMEs.
eligibility: Plant & machinery investment below INR 50 crore
funding_amount: 15% capital subsidy, up to INR 15 lakhs per unit

name: Atal Innovation Mission (AIM) — Tinkering Labs & Incubation
description: Establishes Atal Incubation Centres (AICs) to provide incubation facilities. Grant of up to INR 10 crore over 5 years to selected incubation centres. Startups housed in AICs get mentoring, lab access, and networking.
eligibility: Willingness to be based at an AIC
funding_amount: Non-dilutive incubation support + access to INR 10 crore AIC grant ecosystem

name: SIDBI SMILE Fund
provider: Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI)
description: Provides soft loans and quasi-equity to MSMEs and early-stage startups that are commercially viable but lack collateral. Focus on job creation and inclusive growth sectors.
eligibility: MSME or startup with at least 1 year of operations
Sector agnostic but priority to manufacturing and services
funding_amount: INR 10 lakhs to INR 25 lakhs (soft loan at concessional rates)

name: Digital India Startup Hub
provider: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
description: Connects tech startups with mentors, incubators, investors, and government procurement opportunities. Provides access to MeitY-empaneled incubators and pilot project grants for deep-tech.
eligibility: Product or platform with digital/software component
funding_amount: Pilot grants up to INR 1 crore + incubation support

name: National SC-ST Hub
provider: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
description: Promotes entrepreneurship among SC/ST communities. Provides handholding support, skill development, financial assistance, and access to government procurement for SC/ST-owned enterprises.
eligibility: Desire to participate in government procurement (GeM portal)
funding_amount: Varies — mentoring, skill grants, procurement facilitation

name: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme
description: Incentivizes domestic manufacturing by providing production-linked financial incentives over 4–6 years. Active in 14 sectors including electronics, pharma, food processing, and textiles.
funding_amount: 4–6% of incremental sales above base year for 4–6 years

name: iCreate — International Centre for Entrepreneurship and Technology
description: India's premier startup incubator offering funded residency programs, mentoring, and investor connects. Hosts Indian and international startups across deep-tech, clean-energy, and advanced manufacturing.
eligibility: Open to Indian and foreign startups
funding_amount: Funded residency + access to INR 50 lakh seed fund for selected startups
